scope.summary: Commits the C3 wire-contract fixture: a real X.509 client leaf certificate plus its openssl-computed SHA-256 as a literal, with hand-written operator-style fingerprint spellings asserted against the runtime mtls:sha256:<hex> principal key, and the operator-authority axis committed red for bead oraclemcp-091-b1a-mtls-normalize-eg2il.
out_of_scope: Normalizing http.operator.allowed_subjects at load — B1a (oraclemcp-091-b1a-mtls-normalize-eg2il) owns the fix; this bead owns only the fixture.
The control-listener reset behaviour (B1b drop-log) and the ingress budget (B1c); this fixture asserts authorization outcomes, not socket-level symptoms.
Colon-separated openssl output (AA:BB:...) — that spelling is accepted by neither field, so it is not one of the accepted spellings this rule covers.
Any live TLS handshake: the fixture carries the certificate's identity through the authorization path and never negotiates TLS, so no private key is committed.

entry_points.name: POST /mcp presenting a verified client certificate (http.mtls.client_fingerprints)
entry_points.trace: c3_client_fingerprints_authorize_in_every_accepted_spelling drives handle_http_request for all five spellings and asserts 200 rather than the mtls_client_not_registered 403; c3_an_unrelated_registered_fingerprint_does_not_authorize asserts a different registered certificate still yields that 403

entry_points.name: OperatorAuthorityPolicy::authorize for an mTLS principal (http.operator.allowed_subjects)
entry_points.trace: c3_operator_allowed_subject_authorizes_in_the_canonical_spelling proves the one working spelling; c3_config_load_accepts_every_operator_subject_spelling proves OracleMcpConfig::from_toml_str accepts all five, which is what makes the runtime rejection silent

entry_points.name: certificate-to-fingerprint transcription boundary
entry_points.trace: c3_committed_certificate_hashes_to_the_literal_hex re-derives SHA-256 over the committed DER and asserts it equals the openssl-produced literal and the derived mtls:sha256:<hex> principal key

limitations: Scoped test only: cargo test -p oraclemcp-core --test c3_mtls_literal_fingerprints (5 passed, 1 expected-failure) plus the deliberate red run of that expected failure. The full workspace gate was not run for this commit.

The operator-authority assertion is committed red by design and does not execute in the default run; it is not cited as proof of anything here. Its recorded red output is the deliverable, and B1a flips it green by deleting the #[ignore] with the assertions unchanged.

The operator-authority axis is asserted directly against OperatorAuthorityPolicy::authorize rather than through a live control-listener request. That is the enforcement function the raw == comparison lives in, but it does not exercise the TLS listener, so B1a's own acceptance still needs the rig R1 http lane to complete a real operator API call with an uppercase bare-hex subject.
The certificate is self-signed and its private key was discarded, so this fixture cannot be extended to a real handshake without regenerating the material out of tree.
follow_ups: oraclemcp-091-b1a-mtls-normalize-eg2il must delete the #[ignore] on c3_operator_allowed_subjects_authorize_in_every_accepted_spelling as part of its acceptance, and must not make c3_config_load_accepts_every_operator_subject_spelling red by rejecting the config instead of normalizing it.
